terms.definition: A dimension-less composite index (0 ≤ χ ≤ 1) aggregating relational, institutional, and narrative alignment scores within a population; higher values denote greater system-level integrative order.
In complex-systems theory, a rapid, discontinuous shift from one macro-state to another as control parameters cross critical thresholds.
An operational measure of the number of distinct external information sources (human or algorithmic) that reach an average individual within 24 hours.
A ratio quantifying attentional load (S) as outside voices per day (V) divided by individual cognitive capacity (C). Values S » 1 imply saturation.
The statistical artifact where declining divorce rates appear salutary but actually reflect a shrinking denominator of marriages, masking relational fragility.
Specific application of phase-transition theory to shifts in cultural narrative and institutional coherence.
